channel.title: security on Patrick Armengol
channel.description: Recent content in security on Patrick Armengol

item.title: Security Blogs
item.description: I have compiled a list of security blogs below. For a period of about 3 weeks, I had tried to keep up with these blogs through an RSS feed reader. It was a futile effort and not something I would recommend to the average security person. I ended up spending a majority of my free time reading about criminals doing crime when I would have much rather been learning something new or making something cool.

item.title: Threat Intelligence
item.description: These are my notes on threat intelligence. Intelligence Intelligence is high fidelity information that has been analyzed and can be used to influence decision making. Cyber Threat Intelligence (Cyber (Threat (Intelligence))) Threat Intelligence is Intelligence pertaining to adversaries. Cyber Threat Intelligence is Threat Intelligence pertaining to the cyber domain. CTI involves understanding cyber adversary capabilities, motivations, and goals. CTI finds usefulness in its application to intrusion detection and incident response.

item.title: Stripping Malware Sample Entry Points
item.description: Backstory While going through Malware Data Science, I noticed that the malware samples included in the book’s ‘code and data’ download had their entry points redacted. I’m assuming this was done so that live samples wouldn’t be hosted directly from the site, or so that people working through the book would be unable to accidently infect themselves. I thought it would be a nice exercise to create a tool to do this for a given directory of samples.
